R

read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.BaseFile
Reads the file content from the disk.
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.BinaryFile
 
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.Folder
Reads the contents of the physical directory and populates the children map.
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.medias.Audio
Reads the audio file from disk and initializes the playback Clip.
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.medias.Image
Reads the image file from the disk into the in-memory buffer.
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.TabularFile
Loads tabular data from the physical file into the in-memory buffer.
read() - Method in class basicFiles.files.TextFile
Reads the file content and populates the in-memory line list.
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigBinaryFile
 
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigFile
Reads metadata and rebuilds the index if the file has changed.
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigFolder
 
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigTabularFile
 
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigTextFile
 
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.medias.BigAudio
Reads the audio file and prepares the Clip.
read() - Method in class bigFiles.files.medias.BigImage
 
readPlayWait() - Method in class basicFiles.files.medias.Audio
Reads the file, starts playback, and blocks the current thread until the audio finishes.
readPlayWait() - Method in class bigFiles.files.medias.BigAudio
Reads, plays, and waits until playback finishes.
remove(BaseFile) - Method in class basicFiles.files.Folder
Removes a file or sub-folder from this folder's internal registry.
remove(BigFile) - Method in class bigFiles.files.BigFolder
Removes a child from the internal map.
remove(Object) - Method in class basicFiles.files.TextFile
Removes matching lines from the in-memory buffer based on string equality.
removeRow(int) - Method in class basicFiles.files.TabularFile
Removes the row at the specified index and updates the disk.
rename(String) - Method in class basicFiles.files.BaseFile
Renames the physical file on disk and updates the internal state.
